title: Smoky Brazilian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A meal-prep friendly dish featuring high-protein chicken mixed with smoky flavors and a spicy kick from chili powder and cure for an enjoyable and chewy texture.
instructions: Sprinkle sesame seeds on both sides of the chicken pieces, and sprinkle red pepper flakes, chili powder, and cure evenly over each piece. Let sit for at least 15 minutes.
Season the chicken with salt and pepper before placing it in a baking dish lined with parchment paper.
Bake the chicken for about 20-25 minutes or until fully cooked (internal temperature reaches 165°F).
While the chicken is cooking, heat olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add scallions and garlic to sauté for about 2-3 minutes.
Stir-fry the tomato sauce with the remaining ingredients until it thickens slightly and becomes bubbly, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove chicken from oven and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing. Add slices of chicken into the stir-fry mixture and toss to coat evenly.
Serve hot as a main dish or alongside a side salad.
